title: Vilim Disra
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Moff_disra.jpg]] |name=Vilim Disra |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Specter of the Past]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Color]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue {{C|yellow-flecked}} |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]]{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} *[[Imperial Remnant]] **[[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] **[[Sinister Triumvirate]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Nothing worthwhile is ever gained without risk.|Moff Vilim Disra|}} '''Vilim Disra''' was the [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Politics/Legends|politician]] who was the [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the [[Braxant sector/Legends|Braxant sector]] and member of the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] under the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|military rule]] of [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], the ''de facto'' leader of the [[Imperial Remnant]]. Disra used his powers to be the leader of a secret [[Sinister Triumvirate|triumvirate]] comprised of himself, former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]] [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Grodin Tierce]] and a con-artist named [[Flim (Human)|Flim]]. His cabal was assembled in order to bring the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] into an internal civil war over the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan's]] involvement concerning the [[Caamas Document]] in order to keep Pellaeon, from successfully making peace with the New Republic. Previously, Disra had been a member of the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]] that supported [[Trioculus]]'s claim to the former Imperial throne. ==Biography== ===Early career=== {{Quote|This is Chief Administrator Vilim Disra, Shelkonwa, Shelsha sector. I have an urgent message for the Emperor and Lord Vader. Tell them I've located Princess Leia Organa of Alderaan.|Vilim Disra|[[Allegiance (novel)|''Allegiance'']]}} In [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], Disra became chief administrator of [[Shelsha sector/Legends|Shelsha sector]], under [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Barshnis Choard]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] he double-crossed Choard, who wanted to orchestrate a secession of Shelsa from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. For roughly two years, Disra worked with Choard and a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] named [[Caaldra]] to put together an allegiance of [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] gangs under the [[BloodScar]]s and place them with the resources they would need to take over specific Imperial targets when the time came. Disra also managed to make contacts within the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], pretending that Shelsha would ally with the Rebellion in exchange for the Alliance's help in defending Shelsha from the expected Imperial reprisal. {{Quote|What is your assessment of Disra?"
"He's a con artist and conniver. I wouldn't trust him any farther than I could see him."
"Agreed. I don't intend to.|Darth Vader and Mara Jade|}} However, Disra was planning to betray Choard, Caaldra, and the Rebel Alliance all along. He kept detailed records of everything, so that when the time came he could turn over Choard to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Lord Vader]] and present himself as a hero to the Empire. He succeeded in his plan, made easier because Lord Vader and his [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] were already on Shelsha's capital of [[Shelkonwa]] due to a tip Disra had called in to [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]] informing Vader that Princess [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] was in the city. Though Disra had the [[Skirmish on Shelkonwa|city under lockdown]] to prevent her escape, she managed to do so with the help of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], the [[Hand of Judgment]], and—unwittingly—[[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]. Disra made it clear to Vader that he wished to be named Governor of Shelsha after Choard's removal, but Palpatine refused to do so. ===Imperial Moff=== By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], however, Disra had indeed obtained the rank of Moff. [[Alendar Jarvis]] of the ''[[New Order Progressive]]'', not always a reliable source, reported that he was out of favor with Palpatine, and had been for quite some time, but nonetheless retained good relations with many important supporters of [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Traeda]], and this made it worthwhile for [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Rufaan Tigellinus]] to attempt to recruit him as an ally, as he was attempting to build his own court faction, and working to persuade Traeda's supporters to join it.{{JournalCite|8|Galaxywide NewsNets}} ===Caamas Document Crisis=== Disra later hired the [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]] [[Flim (Human)|Flim]] to portray [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] in order to consolidate Imperial military power under the command of a [[Sinister Triumvirate|triumvirate]] consisting of Disra, Flim, and [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Grodin Tierce]]. Under the nominal leadership of Disra, the triumvirate laid out a plan to sabotage part of the planetary shield of [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]]. Meanwhile, they manipulated hostilities arising from the discovery of a [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] implicating the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] in the post-[[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] bombardment of [[Caamas/Legends|Caamas]].''[[Star Wars: The Hand of Thrawn Duology]]'' Disra also employed the services of pirate gangs, most notably the Cavrilhu gang under Captain [[Zothip]]. The Remnant loaned Zothip's gang leftover clones from Thrawn's campaign against the New Republic 10 years earlier, and in return was supplied with [[Preybird-class starfighter|''Preybird''-class]] starfighters. The ''Preybirds'' were badly needed to augment the dwindling supply of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] in the Imperial Navy, and though far less intimidating, the Preybirds proved to be capable replacements. Disra hired the pirates to attack New Republic shipping convoys, letting Zothip's gang keep anything of value while forcing the New Republic to use valuable resources trying to stop the attacks. Disra used Zothip as a pawn until Zothip arrived at Disra's private office on Bastion with murderous intent. Fortunately for Disra, Zothip was killed by an assassin, a Mistryl Shadow Guard who had also sneaked into his estate. Though Disra was the creator of the secret triumvirate, he never really controlled it. Major Tierce manipulated the group from the beginning. Disra planned betrayal, plotting to kill Tierce and Flim when it came time to do so. He was unaware that Tierce knew of this, but he was in constant disagreement with the soldier, whom he suspected of planning to usurp his leadership. Disra continually attempted to gain the upper hand over him, but never really succeeded. As the first shots over Bothawui were fired, it was revealed that Tierce was, in fact, an unstable clone from a failed project. Disra's triumvirate was laid bare by information procured by smuggler/information broker [[Talon Karrde]] and Disra was arrested on grounds of [[Treason/Legends|treason]]. The Council of Moffs replaced him with [[Ephin Sarreti]]. ==Appearances== *[[Allegiance (novel)|''Allegiance'']] {{Ab|Allegiance (audiobook)}} {{1stID|Vilim|in book}} *{{JournalCite|8|Galaxywide NewsNets}} {{1stm}} *''[[Specter of the Past]]'' {{Ab|Specter of the Past (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *''[[Vision of the Future]]'' {{Ab|Vision of the Future (unabridged audiobook)}} ==Sources== *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{C|under "Gilad Pellaeon"}} {{Imo}} *{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} *{{FactFile|102|CAA 1-2|The Caamas Document}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Moffs19ABY}} {{Interlang |cs=Vilim Disra |de=Vilim Disra |es=Vilim Disra |fi=Vilim Disra |pl=Vilim Disra }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Disra, Vilim}} [[Category:Bastion residents]] [[Category:Bureaucrats of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Moffs of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Moffs of the Imperial Remnant]]
